reasoning:
The question asks if the IMF and Lebanon will announce an economic agreement involving the IMF agreeing to provide money before July 1, 2025. 

Historically, IMF agreements in deeply politically fractured countries like Lebanon take a very long time, as seen when their 2022 staff-level agreement (SLA) fell apart because the government failed to implement structural reforms. 

A newly appointed Lebanese government under PM Nawaf Salam did officially request a new IMF program in February 2025, and an IMF fact-finding mission visited Lebanon in March 2025. Parliament took a positive step by passing a banking secrecy law on April 24, 2025. 

However, on April 28, 2025, Lebanon's Economy Minister stated that they hope to sign an initial staff-level agreement (SLA) "within months." First, an SLA generally does not represent the IMF agreeing to immediately provide money; an SLA only paves the way for IMF Executive Board approval, which happens later *after* prior actions (reforms) are implemented. Second, "within months" from late April easily pushes the timeline past the tight July 1, 2025 deadline. Finally, a May 27, 2025 article in Executive Magazine confirms Lebanon is showing "willingness to re-engage" but does not indicate any final or imminent funding deal has been reached. Therefore, achieving a formal agreement where funds are committed before July 1, 2025 is exceedingly unlikely. I estimate this at 4%.
